denial_pattern_sources: algo_time_delta: ProPublica + Capitol Forum Cigna Investigation 2023; Senate Finance Committee UHC Investigation 2024

denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter advises you of our determination regarding your provider's request on 01/09/2026 for MRI brain with and without contrast related to Headache with new neurologic deficit, subacute (R51.9). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Cigna Medical Coverage Policies and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG). The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. MRI brain with and without contrast with an out-of-network provider is not covered without a gap exception. In-network alternatives are available.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with out of network no authorization. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Headache with new neurologic deficit, subacute (R51.9). He has been treated under commercial coverage with MRI brain with and without contrast recommended as the next appropriate step. New left facial numbness; migraine history but exam changed. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that out of network no authorization bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
